title: Draft: [Part3] ClamAV version is updated to 1.4.3 Resolve DSP-7172
description:
Although the ticket specifies updating the ClamAV version to 1.0.9 (as per the ClamAV documentation in the console), I found that the most recent version is 1.4.3. This is part 3 of the MRs for the luna-bakery repository, and this MR cannot be merged at this time. It can only be merged once we start baking AL2023 AMIs in the production environments.

sh-5.2$ sudo clamscan --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025
sh-5.2$ sudo freshclam --version
ClamAV 1.4.3/27787/Thu Oct  9 09:31:07 2025

title: Draft: [6789] PART 2 - luna-bakery - AL2023 Migration
description:
This MR updates the luna-bakery customization scripts executed during the AMI baking process to support AL2023 AMIs. 

This MR should remain unmerged until we are prepared to start baking AL2023 AMIs for production environments and commence the production migration to AL2023.

title: Draft: [DSP-6573] PART 1 AMI Transition from AL2 to AL2023
description:
There will be numerous merge requests (MRs) for this epic, primarily affecting luna-bakery, ng-infrastructure, terraform-aws-eks module, eks-worker, and eks-cluster module for 1.0 infrastructure. Additional MRs may be required once we complete the IMDS transition from IMDSv1 to IMDSv2 calls. I can only merge all these MRs when every component is ready and the IMDS migration is complete. I've begun raising MRs early to gather reviews and address questions about the changes being implemented for this epic.

This Part 1 of the MR addresses naming convention issues encountered with AL2023 AMIs. The base image name used for AMI filtering has changed from 'eks-node-1.32' to 'eks-node-al2023-x86_64-standard-1.32' in AL2023, which significantly altered the AMI tags and names generated for baked AL2023 AMIs. This MR primarily contains changes to maintain naming conventions consistent with those used for AL2 AMIs. Additionally, since SELinux is set to permissive mode by default in AL2023, I've removed the SELinux script and its associated configuration from the template.

title: DSP-7992: Initial commit, working but kind of unwieldy script
description:
This script isn't exactly code that I'm proud of, but it gets the job done. If you have specific recommendations for cleaning it up, I'll take it, but otherwise as I said in both the ticket and docstring intro I am hoping this is just a stopgap, not needed long-term.

One known deficiency that I haven't felt like spending 15 minutes to change and test: It does not add tenant-level groups, only workspace-level groups (e.g. `ng-abacus-dev-admin` but not `ng-abacus-admin`). This makes it somewhat awkward to run for the Data Copier, which is a tenant-wide app, but in practice everyone in one is in the other.

description:
## Description
Following https://gitlab.com/abacusinsights/abacus-v2/next-gen-platform/ng-infrastructure/-/merge_requests/1075, we now have the ability to generate SSH key secrets as part of deployment of the Airbyte component when needed. In the case of these three clients (Cambia, PHP, and Medstar) this secret has already been generated manually so we'll need to import those resources into Terraform before merging this MR (thus the `Draft` mode.

title: [DSP-8024] Ingest deploy metadata in airflow trigger DAG
description:
## Summary
- Ingest `deploy_metadata` from ADT config in `dag-template.py`.
- Map metadata fields into canonical `SEIJI_SOURCE_*` environment variables for seiji pod execution.
- Keep metadata transport aligned with the orchestrator/souschef naming model.

## Test Plan
- [x] `python3 -m py_compile local-charts/airflow-deploy-trigger/files/dag-template.py`
- [ ] Trigger a dev ADT run and verify `SEIJI_SOURCE_*` vars are present in the seiji pod.

## Ticket
- DSP-8024

title: [DSP-8024] Add deployment metadata injection to hook executors
description:
## Summary
- New `deploy_metadata.py` module that builds `SEIJI_*` env vars (component, version, commit SHA, deploy type, user, source, pipeline URL) plus `TF_VAR_*` equivalents for Terraform
- Injects metadata into all three executors: native (os.environ), docker (`_env` property), kubernetes (pod env update)
- Adds `SEIJI_*/CI_*` passthrough in `generate_kubernetes_environment_variables()` for pre-existing pipeline vars
- Passes `deploy_source` (artifact protocol) from workflow.py through to metadata
- Parses human-readable username from AWS ARN for `SEIJI_DEPLOY_USER`
- Cleans up env vars in `_finalize_execution` to prevent leaking between hooks

**Depends on:** seiji-pkg-framework!67 (provenance stamping)

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests for `deploy_metadata.py` (16 new tests in `test_deploy_metadata.py`)
- [ ] Run full test suite: `just test`
- [ ] Smoke test: local deploy with `env | grep SEIJI_` in a hook script
- [ ] Verify deploys work with old packages (no provenance file)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: [DSP-8024] Stamp build provenance into spack artifacts
description:
## Summary

- Adds `seiji-provenance.json` to spack package zips at build time containing commit SHA, branch ref, repo URL, dirty state, and UTC build timestamp
- Gracefully degrades when git is unavailable (non-git dirs, CI environments without git)
- Part of Phase A: Deployment Metadata Injection for DORA metrics

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `_run_git_command`, `_create_provenance_file` (6 new tests)
- [x] Run full test suite: `just test`
- [x] Verify existing package builds still work
- [x] Verify `seiji-provenance.json` appears in built packages

title: Add deployment metadata injection to souschef pipeline (DSP-8024)
description:
## Summary

- Thread CI provenance metadata (`SEIJI_SOURCE_*` vars) through the child pipeline, `deploy-tenant`, and ADT trigger template so it reaches seiji hook scripts
- Add `deploy_metadata` block to `airflow_deploy_trigger.tpl` carrying commit SHA, pipeline URL/ID, project info, deploy type, and triggered-by
- Create shared Terraform partial (`terraform/seiji-tags-variables.tf`) for downstream hook repos to declare seiji tag variables

## Test plan

- [ ] Dry-run `generate-tenant-jobs` and verify `SEIJI_SOURCE_*` vars appear in `tenant-deployment-pipeline.yml`
- [ ] Set mock env vars and run `generate-adt-config.py`, verify `deploy_metadata` block in output
- [ ] Deploy to a dev tenant and confirm `SEIJI_*` env vars are available in the seiji pod
- [ ] Run `terraform validate` on a minimal config including `seiji-tags-variables.tf`

title: DSP-7995: Seiji-aware helm cache with values detection
description:
## Summary

- **Seiji-aware component scoping**: When `seiji-packaging.yaml` exists, changed files are matched against each helm component's include patterns. Only the desired-state files for affected components are processed (e.g. a values change to `nextgen.eks.deploy` no longer triggers caching for `nextgen.kafka.deploy` in ng-infrastructure)
- **Values detection**: `values/` file changes are now detected as helm-relevant, both in the seiji-aware path and the fallback. Fixes feature branch deploys failing with `chart can't be found` when only values changed
- **index.yaml consistency**: When a `.tgz` already exists in S3, downloads and pushes with `--ignore-if-exists` to ensure `index.yaml` is up to date (fixes retries)
- **Performance optimizations**:
  - Single `yq` pass for `seiji-packaging.yaml` (was N+1 calls per helm component)
  - Batch S3 existence check via single `aws s3 ls` (was N individual calls)
  - Single `yq` pass per desired-state file (was 2)
  - Parallel external chart pulls
  - Skip `helm repo update` when no external charts needed
